AgentProfileConfigUpdate.description:
Per-profile (workspace | support) Workspace Agent settings.

Master admin tunes the system prompt, generation parameters, model, tool
permissions, and excluded tools for each profile independently.

Field validators enforce parameter bounds to prevent invalid LLM configs.
AgentProfileConfigUpdate.title: AgentProfileConfigUpdate

AgentSettings.description: Complete agent settings envelope persisted to ``tenant_agent_settings``.
AgentSettings.title: AgentSettings

AgentSettingsGeneral.description:
Generation parameters a tenant/user may override.

``max_tokens`` is optional: ``None`` means "use the model / profile default".
The numeric fields use sentinel defaults only so the modal can render — at
apply time only non-None values override the profile config.
AgentSettingsGeneral.title: AgentSettingsGeneral

AgentSettingsSystem.description:
Tool and integration exclusion controls for tenants.

System prompts are now master-admin-only. Tenants can only disable
specific tools, MCP servers, and skills from the global catalogue.

Exclusion lists are merged from tenant default and user override
(user exclusions take precedence).

disabled_mcp_servers.description: List of MCP server IDs to disable (global catalogue IDs).
disabled_mcp_servers.title: Disabled Mcp Servers

disabled_skills.description: List of skill slugs to disable (global catalogue slugs).
disabled_skills.title: Disabled Skills

disabled_tools.description: List of tool names to disable (e.g., 'pages_update', 'queryDatasource').
disabled_tools.title: Disabled Tools

AgentSettingsSystem.title: AgentSettingsSystem

ExportRequest.description:
Request body for ``POST /api/edge-engines/{engine_id}/export``.

The passphrase seals the bundle envelope and is its only transit protection for a
cross-deployment move — treat it like a password. It is never stored.

GenericDeployRequest.description:
Provider-agnostic deploy request for the Deploy Engine Wizard.

The endpoint resolves the provider type from provider_id and routes
to the correct deployer. `worker_name` is the resource name
(CF worker, Supabase function, Vercel project, etc.).
GenericDeployRequest.title: GenericDeployRequest

ImportRequest.description:
Request body for ``POST /api/edge-engines/import``.

``bundle`` is the ``FBENG1.…`` string from an export; ``passphrase`` unseals it.
On success the response includes ``confirm_secret`` (S), which the caller pastes
back into the source to finalize the move.
ImportRequest.title: ImportRequest

MoveToProjectRequest.description:

Same-deployment fast path: move the engine to another project in THIS deployment
atomically (no bundle, no passphrase).

SecuritySettings.description:
Security-log IP retention (Post-sprint 2.1 — configurable GDPR strict mode).

Controls how long security audit logs (`audit_logs`) keep the FULL client IP
before it is purged to NULL (the anonymized /24 or /48 value is retained
long-term). Full IPs are needed short-term for new-IP login alerts.

full_ip_retention_days:
  >0  = retain the full IP for this many days, then purge (default 30)
   0  = anonymize immediately (strictest privacy; disables new-IP alerts)
  -1  = retain indefinitely (legitimate-interest mode; never auto-purge)
SecuritySettings.title: SecuritySettings

SettingsResponse.description:
Response for GET /api/agent/settings.

``settings`` is the *effective* merged set (profile → tenant → user) so the
modal shows the user exactly what their next turn will use.
``inherited_from`` describes the most specific layer that contributed.

SettingsUpdate.description:
Request body for PUT /api/agent/settings.

``scope`` selects which row is written: ``user`` (the caller's override) or

Uses asyncio.Semaphore(3) to limit concurrent rotations (each triggers a
redeploy) and avoid provider rate limits. Engines that are not found, not
owned by the caller, or not shared are reported as failed rather than
aborting the batch. See services/edge_secrets_push.rotate_secrets_key.

post.description: Provider-agnostic one-click deploy. Delegates to engine_provisioner.

Import an engine from a sealed bundle into this tenant's project.

Unseals the bundle, remaps IDs, matches-or-creates shared resources, re-encrypts
secrets with the local key, and inserts the engine (inactive, undeployed) in one
transaction. Returns the new engine id, a summary, and the confirm secret S —
paste S back into the source's finalize-move to complete the transfer.

delete.description: Delete an engine record explicitly.

get.description: Get standalone engine by ID with live status.

put.description: Update engine metadata (does not trigger redeploy).

List the tenant-secrets audit trail for an engine (newest first).

Returns control-plane operations (push/delete/rotate) on the engine's
per-tenant secrets, with optional tenant_slug / operation / status filters.
Mirrors the edge-side vault audit, but centralized in the backend DB.
See services/tenant_secrets_audit.py.

parameters.description: Filter to a single tenant slug

schema.title: Tenant Slug

parameters.description: Filter by operation: push | delete | rotate
parameters.name: operation

schema.description: Filter by operation: push | delete | rotate
schema.title: Operation
Response Edge Engines Tenant Secrets Audit Logs

post.description: Cancel a pending move and restore the engine to active.

Export an engine into a sealed, portable bundle and soft-lock it (moved_out).

Cross-deployment move: the full dependency closure (engine + owned children +
shared infra + connected accounts + datasources + storage) is decrypted with the
local key and sealed with the caller's passphrase. The source engine is then
frozen (move_status=moved_out, is_active=False) until the move is finalized or
cancelled. System/shared engines cannot be moved. See
docs/portable-engine-move-plan.md.

Finalize a pending move: verify the confirm secret and delete the source engine.

Constant-time compare of ``sha256(confirm_secret)`` against the stored
``move_secret_hash``. A match proves the target successfully unsealed the bundle and
committed the import — safe to cascade-delete the source (owned children go with it;
shared accounts/infra/datasources/storage stay). Idempotent in the sense that a
repeat call 404s once the engine is gone.

get.description:
Proxy health check to the edge engine with FRONTBASE_SYSTEM_KEY.

Resolves the system key from engine_config (Fernet-encrypted),
calls GET {engine_url}/api/health with x-system-key header,
and returns the full diagnostic response.
List custom domains for this engine.

schema.title: Response Engine Inspector Inspect Engine Domains

post.description:
Add a custom domain to this engine.

Custom domains are intentionally NOT quota-gated: on BYO tiers they're configured via the
tenant's own provider (free, bypassable, no COGS); on managed tiers they're a paid add-on
(`managed_domain`, gated at provisioning via `tenant_addons`). See [TIERS] §4.4.

schema.title: Response Engine Inspector Add Engine Domain

post.description:
Same-deployment fast path (Step 6): move an engine to another project in THIS
deployment atomically — no bundle, no passphrase, no confirm token. Source and
target share the local FERNET_KEY, so transport crypto is unnecessary.

schema.title: Response Edge Engines Move Engine To Project Endpoint

post.description:
Live-reconfigure an engine's DB/cache/queue bindings.

Delegates to engine_reconfigure service (CF Settings API PATCH).
Redeploy an engine with the latest bundle code + current secrets.

Roll back an in-flight key rotation during its transition window.

Restores the previous key (and resolver mode), records the rotation as
'rolled_back' in history, and redeploys. See
services/edge_secrets_push.rollback_rotation.

Rotate a shared engine's per-worker secrets key (zero-downtime).

Generates a new key, re-encrypts every tenant secret under it during the
next deploy, and keeps the old key valid for `window_seconds` (graceful
transition). See services/edge_secrets_push.rotate_secrets_key.

schema.title: Response Edge Engines Rotate Secrets Key

get.description: List key-rotation history for an engine (in-flight transition + past 10).

Provider-agnostic — works for any engine that has been deployed.

schema.title: Response Edge Engines Get Engine Source

put.description:
Save modified source files to the engine's DB snapshot (per-engine isolation).

Payload: { files: { "relative/path.ts": "content", ... } }
Only updates the files provided — other snapshot files remain untouched.

Core Zone Convention:
- Files under frontbase-core/ are tracked in modified_core_files
- Files outside frontbase-core/ set is_forked = True

schema.title: Response Edge Engines Update Engine Source

Fetch /api/manifest from a running engine and sync GPU models + metadata.

Delegates to services/engine_manifest.py.

Hit the engine's /_health route to check code version and connection status.

post.description:
Deploy a new GPU model to an edge engine.

After saving the model record, auto-redeploys CF engines so the AI
binding + FRONTBASE_GPU_MODELS secret are pushed immediately.

schema.title: Response Edge Gpu Create Gpu Model

get.description:
Fetch available models from a GPU provider.

Uses the adapter factory to select the correct implementation.
For CF Workers AI, calls GET /accounts/{id}/ai/models/search.
Return all available I/O schemas by task type.

delete.description:
Delete a GPU model.

After deletion, auto-redeploys CF engines to remove stale AI
binding / secrets.

schema.title: Response Edge Gpu Delete Gpu Model

put.description: Update a GPU model's configuration.

schema.title: Response Edge Gpu Update Gpu Model

post.description: Test a deployed GPU model by running a sample inference.

schema.title: Response Edge Gpu Test Gpu Model

get.description: List all connected edge provider accounts.

post.description:
Create and connect a new edge provider account.

Credentials are encrypted with Fernet AES-256 before storage.
Non-secret metadata (account_id, project_ref) is stored separately for UI display.

get.description:
List database tables from a connected account's credentials.

Resolves credentials directly from EdgeProviderAccount — no sync datasource needed.
Used by the Users panel (Auth Provider → Contacts Database → Table selector).

Supported providers:
- supabase: calls /rest/v1/rpc/frontbase_get_schema_info
- neon: discovers projects then uses SQL via Neon serverless driver
- postgres/mysql: direct connection via info schema (future)

schema.title: Response Edge Providers List Account Tables

post.description: Create a new resource (Redis DB) via Connected Account's management API.

schema.title: Response Edge Providers Create Resource By Account

post.description:
Discover resources (projects, databases, sites) available with the given credentials.

Used during the connect flow to let users pick which project/resource to bind.

schema.title: Response Edge Providers Discover Resources Endpoint

post.description:
Discover resources using stored credentials from a Connected Account.

Decrypts saved credentials server-side and calls the same discovery logic.
For Turso: returns manually-registered databases from stored JSON.
Used by Edge DB/Cache/Queue forms to list available resources.

schema.title: Response Edge Providers Discover By Account

post.description:
Re-validate an existing provider's credentials.

Decrypts stored secrets server-side and calls the same validation logic.

Validate provider credentials by making a lightweight API call.

Does NOT create a record — just verifies the credentials work.
Called before saving to prevent storing invalid tokens.

get.description: Generate a stateless JWT for the Workspace Agent using the active GPU provider.

schema.title: Response Edge Providers Get Workspace Agent Token

post.description: Set a specific provider as the default Workspace Agent provider and generate token.

schema.title: Response Edge Providers Set Workspace Agent Token

post.description:
List engines/functions/apps from a connected edge provider.

Dispatches to provider-specific listing API and returns unified shape.

schema.title: Response Edge Providers List Engines For Provider

post.description: Add a database to a Turso provider account (manual registry).

schema.title: Response Edge Providers Add Turso Database

delete.description: Remove a database from a Turso provider account.

schema.title: Response Edge Providers Remove Turso Database

post.description: Test connection to a specific Turso database within an account.

schema.title: Response Edge Providers Test Turso Database

delete.description: Delete a provider account if no engines depend on it.

get.description: Get a specific edge provider account.

put.description: Update a provider account. Credentials are re-encrypted on change.

get.description:
Return decrypted credentials for a provider account.

Internal endpoint used by the db-synchronizer credential bridge to resolve
datasource credentials from connected accounts. Avoids duplicating
encryption/decryption logic across services.
List all configured edge queues.

post.description: Create a new edge queue connection.
Batch delete queues. Optionally delete remote resources in parallel.
Test a queue connection before saving it.

Delete an edge queue connection.

Fails if any edge engines still reference this queue.
If delete_remote=True and provider supports it, also deletes the remote resource.

get.description: List all configured edge vector stores.

Batch delete multiple edge vector stores.
Test connection using raw fields (pre-save).

schema.title: Response Edge Vectors Test Connection Inline

delete.description:
Delete an edge vector store connection.

If delete_remote=True and the store was created from a Connected Account,
also delete the resource at the provider (e.g. CF Vectorize index).

schema.title: Response Edge Vectors Delete Edge Vector

put.description: Update an existing edge vector store connection.

post.description: Test connection to an existing edge vector store.

schema.title: Response Edge Vectors Test Edge Vector Connection

by profile_slug if provided. Master admins see all servers including disabled ones.

get.description: Get all pages - matches Express: { success, data: pages[] }

post.description: Create a new page - matches Express: { success, data: page }

get.description:
Get the homepage for Edge pull-publish.
Edge calls this when it has no homepage in its local DB.

Get a public page by slug for SSR.
No authentication required - used by Edge Engine.
Returns page data if page exists and is public (or all for now during dev).

delete.description:
Soft delete a page - matches Express: { success, message }.
Unpublishes from ALL active full-bundle Edge Engines.

get.description: Get a page by ID - matches Express: { success, data: page }

put.description: Update a page - matches Express: { success, data: page }
Update page layout - matches Express: { success, data: page }

delete.description:
Permanently delete a page - matches Express: { success, message }.
Unpublishes from ALL active full-bundle Edge Engines, then hard-deletes.

post.description:
Publish a page to multiple Edge Engines in one request.
Serializes the page ONCE, then fans out to all engines in parallel.
Publish a page to a specific Edge Engine target.
Restore a deleted page - matches Express: { success, data: page, message }

Roll back a page to a previous version.
Creates a NEW version snapshot of the current state BEFORE overwriting,
so the rollback itself can be undone.

Unpublish a page from a specific Edge Engine target.
Page remains in the backend DB and on other targets.

get.description: List all version snapshots for a page, newest first.

post.description: Manually create a named version snapshot (e.g., "Pre-launch backup").

get.description: Get a specific version including its full layout_data snapshot.
Public, active plans ordered for a pricing table.
Get project settings

put.description:
Update project settings and sync to Edge for SSR self-sufficiency.
Optimized: Releases DB connection before Edge sync HTTP call.

post.description:
Upload branding assets (favicon, logo) stored locally.
These are independent of user-configured Supabase storage.
Returns a URL path that works in both admin and SSR contexts.

get.description: List projects the current user can access.

post.description: Create a new project (owner/admin only, gated by the plan's `projects` cap).

delete.description: Delete a non-default project (owner only).

get.description:
Datasources for the active project: ``granted`` (shared in, revocable) and
``available`` (tenant datasources not yet in this project, grantable).
List who has access to this project (implicit owners/admins + explicit rows).

post.description: Grant a member access to this project (owner/admin only).

delete.description: Revoke a member's explicit access to this project (owner/admin only).

get.description:
List security events for the calling tenant (read-only).

Filters: event_type, severity, and a created_at date window. ``created_at``
is stored as an ISO-8601 UTC string, so lexicographic comparison is
chronological and the date filters accept plain ``YYYY-MM-DD`` or full
timestamps.

schema.title: Response Security Events List Security Events

get.description:
Lightweight counts by severity for dashboard badges/header chips.

Returns ``{total, by_severity: {low, medium, high, critical}}`` scoped to
the calling tenant (same isolation rules as the list endpoint).

schema.title: Response Security Events Security Events Summary

get.description: Get general site settings

put.description: Update general site settings

post.description: Send an invitation email to a new admin.

get.description:
Get privacy and tracking settings.

Returns configuration for visitor tracking cookies and advanced variables.

put.description:
Update privacy and tracking settings.

Configures visitor tracking behavior:
- enableVisitorTracking: Enable/disable visitor tracking cookies
- cookieExpiryDays: Number of days before tracking cookie expires
- requireCookieConsent: Require user consent before setting tracking cookies

get.description:
Get Redis cache settings.

Returns the explicitly configured Upstash instance from UI if present.
Otherwise, returns the fallback local Redis configuration (powered by env vars).

put.description: Update Redis cache settings.

post.description: Test Redis connection with provided settings.

get.description: Get security-log IP retention settings.

put.description:
Update security-log IP retention.

- full_ip_retention_days > 0 : retain full IP N days, then purge
- 0  : anonymize immediately (strict privacy)
- -1 : retain indefinitely (legitimate interest)

post.description:
Collects anonymized telemetry from self-hosted editions.
Currently mocked to just log locally.

Validates a license key for Enterprise or Community Free upgrades.
Currently mocked to accept any key starting with 'fb_'.

get.description: List all buckets for a storage provider (fast — no size computation).

post.description: Create a new bucket.

delete.description: Delete a bucket (must be empty).

get.description: Get a specific bucket.

put.description: Update bucket settings.

post.description: Empty a bucket.

get.description:
Compute recursive size with L1/L2/L3 caching.

L1: In-memory dict (instant)
L2: Redis with 10-min TTL (survives restart)
L3: Recursive Supabase API walk (expensive, populates L1 + L2)

post.description: Create a folder.

delete.description: Delete files from a bucket.

get.description: List files in a bucket path with global sorting, lazy folder size lookup, and pagination.

parameters.description: Field to sort by (name, size, type, updated_at)
parameters.name: sort_by

schema.description: Field to sort by (name, size, type, updated_at)
schema.title: Sort By

post.description: Move or rename a file.

Move a file across buckets / providers (Sprint 4B + Post-sprint 2.2).

Body:
  source_provider_id, source_bucket, source_key
  dest_provider_id,   dest_bucket,   dest_key

Small files (< 50 MB, or unknown size) are moved synchronously: download from
source → upload to dest → delete source. Large files (≥ 50 MB) are moved by a
background job — this returns ``{async: true, job_id}``` and the client polls
``/api/storage/move-status/{job_id}``. Same-provider adapters with native
server-side copy override ``move_cross`` for efficiency.

get.description: Poll the status of a background cross-bucket move (Post-sprint 2.2).
List Netlify sites for a connected account (used by the site-picker in Add Storage).

post.description:
Create a new Netlify site for storage (reuses netlify_deploy_api.create_site).

After creation, triggers a minimal empty deploy to activate Netlify Blobs.
Without at least one deploy, Blobs write API returns 401 "Access Denied".
